description_long: This is a single-story log cabin with a wide, gently sloped gable roof made of dark shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontally stacked logs, giving it a rustic and traditional appearance. The cabin sits on a circular patch of green terrain, with a stone foundation visible at the base. One side features a covered porch with wooden railings, providing an inviting entryway and outdoor seating area. Windows are symmetrically arranged along the longer facades, and a chimney protrudes from the roof, suggesting a fireplace inside. The overall design is compact and functional, typical of classic log cabins found in rural or park settings.
description: A rustic log cabin with a dark gable roof, stone foundation, and a covered porch, featuring symmetrically arranged windows and a chimney.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entrance, and several windows along the facade.
description_view_1: Side gable view highlighting the roof's slope, chimney, and the solid log wall with minimal openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall with windows and the stone foundation, no porch visible.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with decorative wooden truss detail and no visible windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, dark shingle covering, and slight overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows symmetrically distributed along the longer facades, with no windows on the gable ends; windows are evenly spaced and of similar size.
attribute_door_placement: Single main door located on the front facade, integrated into the covered porch area.
